message:
feat(telegram): integrate SDK schema validation and extract reusable schemas

Wire the new fcp-sdk validation helpers into the Telegram connector so
that every tool invocation validates input against its JSON Schema
before any Telegram API calls, and consolidate inline schema definitions
into reusable static methods.

Schema extraction (connector.rs):
- Extract send_message_input_schema() / send_message_output_schema()
- Extract get_file_input_schema() / get_file_output_schema()
- Extract answer_callback_query_input_schema() / output_schema()
- Extract message_event_schema() for the telegram.message event
- Add input_schema_for(operation) and output_schema_for(operation)
  dispatch methods for runtime schema lookup by operation name
- Replace all inline json!({...}) schema definitions in
  handle_introspect() with calls to the extracted methods, ensuring
  introspection and validation use the same schema source of truth

Validation integration:
- Add fcp-sdk dependency to connectors/telegram/Cargo.toml
- Import validate_input_with_limits and Limits from fcp-sdk
- Call validate_input_with_limits() at the top of validate_input_early()
  with Limits::default() before the existing operation-specific
  validation (e.g., MAX_TEXT_LENGTH check for send_message), providing
  defense-in-depth: the SDK validates schema conformance while the
  connector validates business rules

This is the first reference connector to adopt the SDK validation
helpers introduced in the preceding fcp-sdk commit, serving as the
pattern for other connectors to follow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
